case_title: GENERATION NAILS A SOLE PROPRIETORSHIP VS. SENTINEL INSURANCE COMPANY LIMITED, A CORPORATION

docket_entries.description: NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF
CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 100039990) FILED BY DEFENDANT SENTINEL INSURANCE COMPANY, LTD. JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 6.0 DAYS
SUBSTITUTION OF ATTORNEY, (TRANSACTION ID # 100039960): ECHEVERIA, RICARDO SUBSTITUTED FOR KORNBLUM, GUY ORVILLE AS ATTORNEY FOR GENERATION NAILS A SOLE PROPRIETORSHIP
REPLY IN SUPPORT OF ITS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 100056663) FILED BY DEFENDANT SENTINEL INSURANCE COMPANY, LTD.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SENTINEL INSURANCE COMPANY, LTD.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT PLACING MR. ILAOA'S FEET IN THE WATER BASIN WAS AN INSEPARABLE PART OF THE PEDICURE PROCESS AND THE PROFESSIONAL SERVICES EXCLUSION APPLIES AS A MATTER OF LAW. (SEE 16 CCR 977; 980.1-980.4.) THE FOOTBATH WAS A PROFESSIONAL SERVICE IN THE SENSE THAT IT CONSTITUTED AN ASPECT OF THE NAIL SALON PROFESSION AND WAS DONE FOR FINANCIAL GAIN. THERE IS NO AMBIGUITY IN THE EXCLUSION. THE INJURY AROSE DURING THE RENDERING OF SKIN ENHANCEMENT AND GROOMING SERVICES AND THE FOOTBATH WAS NOT "AN ACTIVITY INCIDENTALLY RELATED TO [PLAINTIFFS'] EVERYDAY OPERATIONS." (HOLLINGSWORTH V. COMMERCIAL UNION INS. CO. (1989) 208 CAL.APP.3D 800, 808; SEE ALSO ANTLES V. THE AETNA CASUALTY AND SURETY COMPANY (1963) 221 CAL.APP.2D 438 (CHIROPRACTIC PATIENT BURNED BY DEFECTIVE HEAT LAMP NOT COVERED BY CGL POLICY BECAUSE IT OCCURRED DURING THE RENDERING OF PROFESSIONAL SERVICES).) ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/HEK)

DECLARATION OF TED SMITH IN SUPPORT OF SENTINELS OPPOSITION TO PLAINTIFFS MOTION TO STAY (TRANSACTION ID # 100041326) FILED BY DEFENDANT SENTINEL INSURANCE COMPANY, LTD.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS GENERATION NAILS, GENERATION NAILS, LLC, THUONG HOANG AND ANH NGUYEN'S MOTION FOR STAY OF PROCEEDINGS IS GRANTED IN PART. EFFECTIVE IMMEDIATELY ALL PROCEEDINGS IN THIS CASE, INCLUDING ALL DISCOVERY, ARE STAYED UNTIL THE CONCLUSION OF THE UNDERLYING CASE WITH THE SOLE EXCEPTION THAT DEFENDANT SENTINEL INSURANCE COMPANY MAY FILE AND HAVE HEARD A SUMMARY MOTION LIMITED TO THE ISSUE OF WHETHER IT OWES A DUTY TO DEFEND. BALANCING FAIRNESS TO BOTH SIDES AND FIDELITY TO CASE LAW FAVORS THE LIMITED EXCEPTION TO THE STAY PROVIDED FOR IN THIS ORDER. COUNSEL FOR SENTINEL IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M.GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Sentinel Insurance Company, Ltd.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities
Off Calendar Dec-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Steven M. Schuetza, Esq., Shernoff Bidart Echeverria,telephone 909-621-4935, appearing for Plaintiff

Appearances
Stevven M. Schuetze, Esq., Shernoff Didart Echeverria, Telephone 909-621-4935, appearing for Plaintiffs;
Ann K. Johnston, Esq., Johnston Smith, Telephone 415-891-3321, appearing for Defendant Sentinel Insurance Co.,;
Landon R. Schwob, Esq., Cruser, Mitcehll, Novitz, Sanchez, Gaston, Telephone 213-689-8500, appearing for Defendant Insurance Pro Agencies (CourtCall);

RULING - ARGUED: The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Sentinel Insurance Company, Ltd.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. The undisputed evidence shows that placing Mr. Ilaoa's feet in the water basin was an inseparable part of the pedicure process and the professional services exclusion applies as a matter of law. (See 16 CCR 977; 980.1-980.4.) The footbath was a professional service in the sense that it constituted an aspect of the nail salon profession and was done for financial gain. There is no ambiguity in the exclusion. The injury arose during the rendering of skin enhancement and grooming services and the footbath was not "an activity incidentally related to [plaintiffs'] everyday operations." (Hollingsworth v. Commercial Union Ins. Co. (1989) 208 Cal.App.3d 800, 808; see also Antles v. The Aetna Casualty and Surety Company (1963) 221 Cal.App.2d 438 (chiropractic patient burned by defective heat lamp not covered by CGL policy because it occurred during the rendering of professional services).) Order is signed

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/HEK)

Off Calendar Aug-10-2018 Continued To Feb-27-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS
Off Calendar Jul-17-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF GENERATION NAILS, THUONG HOANG, ANH NGUYEN's MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS.

Plaintiffs Generation Nails, Generation Nails, LLC, Thuong Hoang and Anh Nguyen's motion for stay of proceedings is granted in part. Effective immediately all proceedings in this case, including all discovery, are stayed until the conclusion of the underlying case with the sole exception that defendant Sentinel Insurance Company may file and have heard a summary motion limited to the issue of whether it owes a duty to defend. Plaintiffs may oppose the motion on any grounds they see fit, including that resolution of the motion requires determination of facts that overlap with facts that are in dispute in the underlying action and thus the court should refrain from ruling on the motion until after the conclusion of the underlying case. The only basis for opposition of this stay motion is Sentinel's contention that it should not have to wait till the conclusion of the underlying action to establish that it does not owe a duty to defend and that it can do so without need for determination of any facts that overlap with facts that are in dispute in the underlying action. Sentinel's position appears to be correct, but it may turn out that a summary motion filed by it shows that its position is incorrect. Balancing fairness to both sides and fidelity to case law favors the limited exception to the stay provided for in this order.

Counsel for Sentinel is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
